fields.description:
# WS4 — Gate dispatch entry + source separation

## Why
The capability must be OFF by default and SOURCE-SEPARATED so it is inert when disabled, while preserving the existing public surface. This story adds the dispatch entry (parallel to plan-review) and isolates the code into its own optional module, retiring the throwaway single-pass route.

## What
- `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict(ctx, cfg, ...)` running `gates/code-review.yaml` in-memory (parallel to `produce_plan_review_verdict`), returning the typed `code_review_verdict` (`{verdict, blocking[], advisory[], coaching[], coverage}`) + a review sidecar.
- Source-separate `code_review.py` into an optional `rebar.llm.code_review` package; PRESERVE the public surface (the `review_code(...)` callable, the MCP `review_code` tool, the `rebar review-code` CLI keep names/signatures and `review_result` output) via re-export/shim.
- RETIRE the single-pass `evidence + aggregate_findings`-only route (unreachable); record the one-way-door removal in an ADR.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` — `produce_code_review_verdict`.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/__init__.py` — package + re-export shim.
- `src/rebar/mcp_server.py`, `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` — surface unchanged.
- `docs/adr/` — ADR for retiring the single-pass demo.
- OUT OF SCOPE: detectors (WS5), Gerrit (WS6).

## Specifics pinned (plan-review remediation 2026-06)
- **`code_review_verdict` is a REGISTERED, typed output schema** (in `src/rebar/schemas/`), mirroring plan-review's verdict: `{verdict: PASS|BLOCK, blocking: [finding], advisory: [finding], coaching: [note], coverage: {det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls}}`. `finding`/`note` reuse the existing shared shapes.
- **Sidecar mechanism:** emitted via the SAME `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar path plan-review uses (an `emit_sidecar` on the target), not a new format.
- **"inert when disabled" observable:** a test asserts that with the feature flag OFF, `produce_code_review_verdict` returns the disabled/no-op result and makes ZERO LLM calls (FakeRunner call-count == 0).
- **Back-out:** flag defaults OFF + the re-export shim preserves the public surface; reverting the flag restores prior behavior. The ADR records this one-way door and its back-out.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `produce_code_review_verdict` runs the gate on the v3 engine and returns the typed `code_review_verdict` + emits a sidecar; OFF by default (config/env gated), inert when disabled.
- [ ] The public surface is preserved: `rebar.llm.review_code(...)`, MCP `review_code`, and `rebar review-code` keep names/signatures/`review_result`; a test exercises each.
- [ ] The single-pass GATE PATH is unreachable (no `evidence + aggregate_findings`-only route runs); an ADR records the removal.
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` + the test suite pass.

## Plan-review remediation (Round-11, 2026-06-29)
- Disabled-path shim return PINNED + one-way-door contradiction resolved: the single-pass code is DELETED, so reverting the feature-flag default does NOT restore single-pass behavior — it makes the gate INERT. When OFF, the preserved `review_code()` surface returns a valid empty `review_result` (zero findings + a 'capability disabled' note), NEVER an error or stub. The ADR states honestly that retirement is a one-way door and the flag-default is the inertness control, not a behavior-restore (this supersedes any 'revert the flag restores prior behavior' phrasing).
- Stale-reference audit: WS4 audits + updates docs / CLI help / `docs/` mentions of the retired single-pass flow.

Added ACs:
- [ ] When disabled, `review_code()` returns a valid empty `review_result` (+ 'disabled' note), not an error/stub; a test exercises the disabled path
- [ ] ADR states retirement is a one-way door; reverting the flag yields INERT (empty review_result), not restored single-pass behavior
- [ ] Stale references to the single-pass `code_review.py` flow in docs/CLI-help/`docs/` are audited + updated
